data.title: AAPL Stock Jumps 3% as "People see Apple as a safe haven"
data.description:
Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares rose more than 3% on Tuesday after the company announced a March 4 product event. The gain made Apple the top performer among the Magnificent Seven stocks yesterday. While shares of Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOGL) slipped, Apple moved higher as investors looked for steadier ground in tech.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company invited media to what it called a "special Apple Experience," scheduled for 9:00 AM ET in New York, London, and Shanghai. The invitation provided few details, which often adds to market interest. At the same time, reports suggest Apple may roll out several new devices during the week of March 2.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs ahead of Interest Rate Clues from Fed Minutes
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened Wednesday's trading session in the green ahead of the release of January's Fed meeting minutes.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last month, the central bank voted 10-2 to hold interest rates steady between 3.50% and 3.75%. Since then, members of the Fed have issued divided opinions, with some signaling support for reductions to benefit the labor market and others calling for a pause in order to address the risk of inflation.

Next FOMC Meeting Set for March 18 as Market Eyes PCE Inflation

The Fed is set to meet for its next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18, where it is widely expected to keep rates unchanged. Traders price in 94.1% odds of this scenario, according to the CME FedWatch tool. By year-end, the Fed's most likely outcome is to cut rates twice, or by 50 bps.

On Friday, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will publish December's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index, providing the central bank with a key update on inflation. Core PCE, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation and is expected to come in at 2.8%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Here's Why Rackspace Technology Stock (RXT) Soared Today
data.description:
Rackspace Technology (RXT) stock underwent a massive rally on Wednesday after the cloud technology services company announced a strategic partnership with big data company Palantir Technologies (PLTR). This collaboration will have Rackspace Technology assist enterprises with the deployment and operation of Palantir's Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rackspace Technology noted that, with its assistance, customers of Palantir will be able to make use of the latter's AI software in a matter of weeks or months rather than months or years. Rackspace Technology will also run Palantir software in its secured Private Cloud and UK Sovereign data centers, allowing regulated customers to make use of Palantir's services.

Gajen Kandiah, CEO of Rackspace Technology, said, "Organizations need AI that works in production, not just in demos. Palantir's platform, combined with Rackspace's governed cloud operations and our shared forward deployed engineering approach, enables customers to accelerate time to value and drive competitive business impact with governance and security. This is especially important in regulated industries."

Rackspace Technology Stock Movement Today

Rackspace Technology stock was up 145.82% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a slight dip yesterday. The shares have fallen 56.8% year-to-date and 85.55% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of RXT stock, as more than 80 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 1.02 million units.
data.author: William White

Global X MSCI China Consumer Discretionary ETF (CHIQ)

Global X MSCI China Consumer Discretionary ETF allows investors to tap into China's growing consumer spending boom. It tracks the MSCI China Consumer Discretionary 10/50 Index, focusing on companies in autos, apparel, textiles, services, and leisure. As China's middle class expands and demands lifestyle upgrades, CHIQ offers targeted exposure to these trends in the world's largest population. CHIQ carries an expense ratio of 0.65%.

According to TipRanks' unique ETF analyst consensus, determined based on a weighted average of analyst ratings on its holdings, CHIQ is a Moderate Buy. The Street's average price target of $27.22 implies an upside of 29.5%.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Fed Minutes, NVDA and Tech Stocks Gain
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Wednesday morning ahead of the minutes from the Federal Reserve's January meeting. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.49%, 0.39%, and 0.26%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EST on February 18.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher.  The S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq Composite all edged up roughly 0.1% on the day. The software sector, battered by AI disruption fears, dropped further yesterday. CrowdStrike (CRWD) tumbled 3.6%, while ServiceNow (NOW) shed 1.1%.

Turning to economic data, traders are focused on the FOMC minutes due later today. Also, data on housing starts, building permits, and durable goods orders will be published today. Of more importance is Friday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index for December, the Fed's favorite gauge, which will reveal more about the economy's health.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) were up 2% in pre-market trading, driven by its expanded partnership with Meta Platforms (META). Meanwhile, Palo Alto Networks (PANW) stock fell 6% after issuing weak earnings guidance for the current quarter.

Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock was up 3% in pre-market trading after Mizuho upgraded the AI-powered data analytics company to Buy from Hold, citing an improved risk-reward set-up. In contrast, Analog Devices (ADI) stock was up about 6% after reporting better-than-expected revenue and earnings for Q1 FY26.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
